concept.conceptName: Inspired minute Volume during Mechanical ventilation
Expired minute Volume during Mechanical ventilation

en.name: Minute volume
en.shortDescription: Minute volume (minute ventilation) — gas volume moved per minute, in L/min (total, inspired/expired, spontaneous/mandatory, setting).
en.longDescription:
## Definition & Clinical Context

Minute volume (minute ventilation) is the total volume of gas moved in or out of the lungs per minute — tidal volume multiplied by respiratory rate. It reflects how much the patient is being ventilated. In source data it is a single numeric value per patient-timestamp, measured or set by the ventilator. It is recorded in intensive care and anaesthesia for ventilated patients. It is reported in litres per minute (L/min).

## Included Concepts

The set lists the SNOMED and LOINC concepts for minute volume, grouped by what they qualify. All are reported in L/min.

### Generic minute volume (default)

- *4353621 / 250811004 — Minute volume (SNOMED)*: the general concept; the default.
- *4107999 / 250812006 — Total minute volume (SNOMED)*: total (spontaneous + mandatory) minute volume.

### By phase (inspired / expired)

- *4141207 / 426102006 — Inspiratory minute volume (SNOMED)*.
- *21490754 / 75960-5 — Inspired minute Volume (LOINC)*.
- *42527121 / 76009-0 — Inspired minute Volume during Mechanical ventilation (LOINC)*.
- *4144329 / 426168008 — Expiratory minute volume (SNOMED)*.
- *42527120 / 76008-2 — Expired minute Volume during Mechanical ventilation (LOINC)*.

### By breath origin

- *4108000 / 250813001 — Spontaneous minute volume (SNOMED)*: from the patient's own breaths.
- *4108447 / 250815008 — Mandatory minute volume (SNOMED)*: from ventilator-delivered breaths.
- *4353945 / 250814007 — Triggered minute volume (SNOMED)*.

### Setting

- *3045410 / 33425-0 — Minute volume setting Ventilator (LOINC)*: the set (target) minute volume, e.g. for mandatory-minute-ventilation modes.

**Note on units**: reported in **L/min** (`8698 / L/min — liter per minute`, UCUM), the recommended unit.

## Mapping Notes

- **Default concept** when the source records a minute volume without qualification: *4353621 / 250811004 — Minute volume*.
- **Expired is the usual measured value**: ventilators typically report expired minute volume; map to an expiratory/expired concept when the source specifies it, otherwise use the generic concept.
- **Setting vs measured**: map the set (target) minute volume to *3045410 / 33425-0 — Minute volume setting Ventilator*; measured values go to the measured concepts.
- **Spontaneous vs mandatory**: map to the spontaneous, mandatory or triggered concept only when the source distinguishes the breath origin.
- Values in mL/min must be converted to L/min (÷1000) before mapping.
